name: Curucuta Route
name_original: Ruta Curucuta por el Pico San Juan — vuelta a la comarca de Sanabria
description: This difficult 34.4-mile mountain biking route in Sanabria offers panoramic views, diverse forests, and a challenging ride for experienced c
region_intro: Mountain biking around Asturianos offers routes through the varied landscapes of the Sanabria region, characterized by its natural park, reservoirs, and the Sierra de la Culebra mountain range. The terrain includes rolling hills, forested sections, and open areas around water bodies. Riders can expect a mix of ascents and descents across these natural features. Best mountain bike trails around Asturianos The most popular mountain bike trail is Curucuta Route, a 34.4 miles (55.3 km) trail that takes 5 hours 26 minutes to complete, featuring challenging climbs and varied terrain. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Alto de San Juan and Palacios de Sanabria Loop, a difficult 31.7 miles (50.9 km) path that explores high points and scenic villages. Local mountain bikers also love the Linarejos and Rihonor de Castilla from Robledo — circular route through the Sierra de la Culebra, a 28.3 miles (45.6 km) trail leading through the Sierra de la Culebra, often completed in about 4 hours 10 minutes. Mountain biking around Asturianos is defined by mountain ranges like the Sierra de la Culebra, natural park areas, and reservoir views. The network offers options for moderate to difficult ability levels. The routes in Asturianos are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 8 reviews. More than 70 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Asturianos's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails around Asturianos?
A: Mountain biking around Asturianos offers a diverse experience through the varied landscapes of the Sanabria region. You'll encounter rolling hills, dense forested sections, and open areas, particularly around reservoirs. The routes often feature a mix of ascents and descents across these natural features, including parts of the Sierra de la Culebra mountain range.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or those looking for an easier ride?
A: While the Asturianos area is known for its moderate to difficult mountain bike trails, with 12 moderate and 3 difficult routes listed, there are no trails specifically categorized as easy in the immediate vicinity. However, the broader Asturias region offers 'Greenways' like the Senda del Oso, which are converted disused railway lines, ideal for more relaxed rides and families. For routes directly around Asturianos, expect a good workout.

Q: What are some challenging mountain bike trails for experienced riders in Asturianos?
A: Experienced riders looking for a challenge can tackle routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1018857698/curucuta-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Curucuta Route&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 55.3 km trail with over 1170 meters of elevation gain. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1222869418/alto-de-san-juan-and-palacios-de-sanabria-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de San Juan and Palacios de Sanabria Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is 50.9 km long and explores high points and scenic villages with over 1000 meters of climbing.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in the Asturianos area?
A: Yes, several mountain bike routes around Asturianos are circular. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1222097394/linarejos-and-rihonor-de-castilla-from-robledo-circular-route-through-the-sierra-de-la-culebra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Linarejos and Rihonor de Castilla from Robledo — circular route through the Sierra de la Culebra&lt;/a&gt;, a 45.6 km moderate trail. Another circular route is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1222894531/puebla-de-sanabria-desde-robledo-circular-por-al-embalse-de-cernadilla" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puebla de Sanabria desde Robledo — circular por al Embalse de Cernadilla&lt;/a&gt;, a 51.1 km moderate ride around the Cernadilla reservoir.

Q: What natural features or scenic viewpoints can I expect to see while mountain biking near Asturianos?
A: The region around Asturianos is characterized by mountain ranges like the Sierra de la Culebra, natural park areas, and reservoir views. While specific viewpoints are not detailed for every route, the trails offer glimpses of the varied geography of the Sanabria region, including its lakes and forests. The broader Asturias region is known for its dramatic landscapes, from the rugged Picos de Europa to stunning coastlines.

Q: What landmarks or attractions can I explore near the mountain bike trails in Asturianos?
A: While mountain biking, you can explore several interesting landmarks nearby. Consider visiting the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/910086" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of the Counts of Benavente&lt;/a&gt; or the charming &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3834068" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Town of Puebla de Sanabria&lt;/a&gt;. For religious sites, there's the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3866268" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of the Incarnation&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5458087" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of Our Lady of Remedies, Otero de Sanabria&lt;/a&gt;. Many routes also offer scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6142104" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;viewpoints&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Asturianos?
A: The mountain bike trails around Asturianos are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 8 reviews. Riders often praise the varied terrain, which includes challenging climbs and scenic passages through the Sanabria region's natural park, reservoirs, and the Sierra de la Culebra mountain range.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike options in the Asturianos area?
A: The mountain bike trails directly around Asturianos are generally categorized as moderate to difficult, which might be challenging for younger children or less experienced family members. For more relaxed, family-friendly rides, the broader Asturias region features 'Greenways' (Vías Verdes) such as the Senda del Oso, which are disused railway lines converted into easy cycling paths, winding through natural parkland.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Asturianos?
A: The best time to go mountain biking in Asturianos generally aligns with the spring and autumn months when temperatures are milder and the weather is more stable. Summers can be warm, especially in open areas, while winters can bring colder temperatures and potentially snow in higher elevations, making some trails less accessible. Always check local weather conditions before heading out.

Q: Are there any mountain bike trails that pass by lakes or reservoirs in Asturianos?
A: Yes, the region around Asturianos is characterized by its reservoirs. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1222894531/puebla-de-sanabria-desde-robledo-circular-por-al-embalse-de-cernadilla" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puebla de Sanabria desde Robledo — circular por al Embalse de Cernadilla&lt;/a&gt; route, for example, offers a scenic ride around the Cernadilla reservoir, providing beautiful water views as you cycle.

Q: How long are the mountain bike trails around Asturianos?
A: The mountain bike trails around Asturianos vary in length. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1018857698/curucuta-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Curucuta Route&lt;/a&gt; is 55.3 km (34.4 miles) long, typically taking around 5 hours 26 minutes to complete. Another route, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1018903813/muelas-de-los-caballeros-y-pena-de-la-bandera-circular-desde-espadeno" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Muelas de los Caballeros y Peña de la Bandera — circular desde Espadeño&lt;/a&gt;, is shorter at 26.2 km (16.3 miles) and can be completed in about 2 hours 30 minutes.

Q: Is it possible to find accommodation for cyclists in the Asturianos area?
A: While specific cyclist-friendly accommodations are not detailed in the provided data, the region of Asturias is a popular destination for outdoor activities, including cycling. You can typically find various accommodation options in nearby towns and villages that cater to tourists and outdoor enthusiasts. It's advisable to check for places that offer secure bike storage or other cyclist-specific amenities when booking.

faqs.question: Is the Ruta Curucuta por el Pico San Juan trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this trail is considered difficult and is best suited for experienced mountain bikers with very good physical condition. You should be prepared for sections where you may need to push your bike due to steep climbs or technical terrain.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Ruta Curucuta mountain bike trail?
faqs.answer: The route features a diverse range of traditional paths and trails, winding through beautiful and varied forest masses. You'll encounter oak, yew, holly, riverside forests, meadows, and pine forests, with areas carpeted in ferns. Expect a mix of surfaces and technical challenges.

faqs.question: What viewpoints and landmarks can I see along the Ruta Curucuta trail?
faqs.answer: You'll be treated to breathtaking views of the Sierra de la Culebra, La Segundera, La Carballeda, and Lake Sanabria. The Alto de San Juan provides unique panoramic vistas. Notable points include the Mirador de Peñas Surrapias and the Ermita de Santo Cristo de Palacios de Sanabria. The trail also descends to the Mozarabic Sanabrés Way.

faqs.question: What wildlife might I encounter on this route?
faqs.answer: The Sanabria region is rich in wildlife. Along the Ruta Curucuta, you have the chance to spot wolves, deer, wild boars, and stags, particularly during the autumn rut.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike the Ruta Curucuta?
faqs.answer: While enjoyable in many seasons, autumn is particularly rewarding for wildlife spotting, especially for seeing stags during their rut. Generally, spring and early autumn offer pleasant temperatures for mountain biking in the region.

faqs.question: Where can I park and access the trailhead for this route?
faqs.answer: The starting and ending point for the Ruta Curucuta is in Asturianos. This town is well-equipped for cyclists, offering convenient parking facilities.

faqs.question: Is the Ruta Curucuta por el Pico San Juan trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While dogs are generally allowed on trails in the Sanabria region, it's always recommended to keep them on a leash, especially given the presence of wildlife like wolves and deer. Ensure your dog is fit enough for a challenging, long ride.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to ride this trail or enter Sanabria Lake Natural Park?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits required or entrance fees to access the Ruta Curucuta trail or to enter the Sanabria Lake Natural Park and the Segundera and Porto mountain ranges where it is located. However, always respect local regulations and private property.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several other paths. You'll ride along parts of the Camino Sanabrés (East), Mtb Camino Sanabrés (Este) / Mtb Camiño Xabrés (Leste), Pista del Cimbrio, Pista de Santiago, and Pista a los Repetidores, among others.

faqs.question: Are there amenities available near the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the starting and ending point in Asturianos is well-equipped. You'll find a restaurant, a sports hall, a pilgrim's hostel, a picnic area, a public barbecue, bike parking, a pharmacy, and a supermarket, making it convenient for pre- and post-ride needs.

faqs.question: What cultural and historical aspects does the Ruta Curucuta offer?
faqs.answer: The route passes through towns like Asturianos, Palacios de Sanabria, Cervantes-Robleda, and Rosinos de la Requejada, showcasing significant examples of traditional Sanabrian architecture. The name "Curucuta" itself is derived from the ancient Asturian Zoela tribes. The trail also includes a descent to the historic Sanabrian Way of Saint James (Camino Mozárabe).

waypoints.name: Ermita de Santo Cristo de Palacios de Sanabria
